repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_completeness: banked operator-spectrum proposal did not give this finite weighted-array dimension formula.

published_prior_art.cite: YuShiTchetgen2025 defines H_gamma and gives only a K=2 illustration; MiaoGengTchetgen2018 gives known-valid-proxy rank conditions, not this fortified supply decomposition.

repo_prior_art.cite: No catalogue entry proves a universal finite-support obstruction for the fortified-completeness pairing.

published_prior_art.cite: YuShiTchetgen2025 assumes fortified completeness for its identification route; ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 studies nonunique bridge solutions rather than this supply-demand obstruction.
A direct monotonicity corollary of the proposed dimension formula; no cited paper states this cardinality frontier.

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_completeness identifies finite realization as unresolved but does not formulate this compatible fortified-pairing minor route.

published_prior_art.cite: AllmanMatiasRhodes2009 and Gu2025BlessingDependence give generic-identifiability techniques for different constrained latent models, not generic row-rank attainment of this fortified pairing.

structure_flags.label: cluster: scm
structure_flags.one_line: This is a potential-outcomes proximal-completeness feasibility result, not a graph-native SCM identification kernel; reclassify it into the appropriate identification/methodological lane.

novelty_flags.label: Drafter comparator promise table / Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The prose says the universal barrier strictly tightens YuShiTchetgen2025, but the required comparator-promise table is absent; add the anchored comparator claim and the strict route-level comparison.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2 (universal rank barrier)
novelty_flags.one_line: It attributes fortified outcome completeness to “Theorem 1” of YuShiTchetgen2025, but the cited preprint states this as Assumption 5 and uses it in Theorem 2.1.

soundness_flags.label: Assumption ass:exact-cardinality-validity
soundness_flags.one_line: YuShiTchetgen2025 assumes |nu-star| >= gamma, not the proposal’s exact equality alpha-star=alpha in A_gamma; retag this as an explicitly defined restricted component rather than a standard cited condition.

soundness_flags.label: Assumptions ass:full-cell-support and ass:latent-stratum-treatment-positivity
soundness_flags.one_line: Full-cell support P(U,A,Z)>0 at every cell already implies 0<P(A=a|U,C_alpha-star)<1, so retain both only after weakening the support condition or remove the redundant positivity assumption from the exact finite model.

soundness_flags.label: Open-ended Question 1 / def:compatible-minor-handle
soundness_flags.one_line: The handle enforces validity of alpha but never defines or imposes maximal invalidity outside alpha, so it does not define the claimed exact-cardinality alpha-star=alpha completion space.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 2 / technical_internal_limitation
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Negative slack is accepted only as failure of the anchor’s fortified outcome-completeness route, not as nonidentification of the ATE.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: For an exactly specified valid-proxy component and a full-cell finite law, negative slack precludes injectivity of the stated pairing; no query-level ATE nonidentification follows.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 1 / honest_scope
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Nonnegative slack is not assumed sufficient; compatible-completion rank attainment remains open.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The settled result is a necessary arithmetic barrier and its monotone frontier, conditional on the restricted exact-cardinality component being formally defined.

source_verification_receipts.locator: pp. 8–12, Assumption 3, equation (2.3), Assumption 5, Remark 2.3, and Theorem 2.1
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: Assumption 3 is |nu-star|>=gamma; Assumption 5 is the fortified-completeness pairing involving H_gamma+L2(Z_-nu-star); the resulting ATE identification theorem is numbered Theorem 2.1.
The article states that at least two independent proxy variables satisfying a rank condition can nonparametrically identify the causal effect.
The article studies existence and nonuniqueness of proximal integral-equation solutions and gives solution-set and efficient counterfactual-mean inference under additional regularity.

source_verification_receipts.version: Published Annals of Statistics 37(6A), 2009, pp. 3099–3132; publisher reprint
source_verification_receipts.locator: p. 1 abstract; pp. 2–6 discussion of generic identifiability and algebraic exceptional sets
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper develops algebraic generic-identifiability arguments for latent-structure models, centered on Kruskal-type tensor results, and explains why dimension equality alone need not establish identifiability.

source_verification_receipts.version: Bernoulli 31(2), 2025, pp. 948–972; publisher access was unavailable to WebFetch, corroborated by arXiv:2203.04403v6
source_verification_receipts.locator: Publisher article metadata; arXiv v6 pp. 1–2
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The published paper concerns discrete models with multiple binary latent variables and gives BLESS-model generic-identifiability criteria whose boundary behavior depends on latent dependence; it is methodologically relevant but not a fortified-proxy rank theorem.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of the American Statistical Association 119(547), 2024, pp. 1778–1793
source_verification_receipts.locator: p. 1778 abstract; p. 1791 discussion of Theorem 1 and polynomial-program extrema
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The article reduces discrete causal bounding problems to polynomial programs and supplies an automated sharp-bounding approach, rather than an analytic feasibility frontier for fortified completeness.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define alpha-star as the maximal set satisfying the proxy-validity restrictions, then encode failure of validity for every coordinate outside alpha (or consistently relabel the target as a cardinality-gamma valid subset rather than an exact set).
Add the missing comparator-promise table and cite YuShiTchetgen2025 Assumption 5, Remark 2.3, and Theorem 2.1; remove the incorrect “Theorem 1” reference.

Give an explicit compatible positive table and a nonzero maximal minor for a small K,m,u instance, then state the generic-attainment program as an iff feasibility theorem rather than only an open handle.

recommended_next_step: Revise the exact-component definition, redundant support/positivity assumptions, comparator documentation, and cluster classification, then rerun Stage -0.5.
